subject: Geography
title: Ice Core Records
description: A short lesson to help you apply Ice Core Records.

hook.text: Air bubbles trapped in Antarctic ice preserve atmospheric samples from 800,000 years ago.

buildup.text: Each layer of ice records a year of snowfall, temperature, volcanic dust, and gas composition.

discovery.text: Ice cores revealed that CO₂ and temperature have risen and fallen together through every ice age.

twist.text: Today's CO₂ levels are higher than anything recorded in those 800,000 years of ice.

climax.text: Ice cores prove that we've pushed the atmosphere into territory it hasn't seen in nearly a million years.

punchline.text: The ice remembered everything. The message is clear.

quiz.question: What do ice core air bubbles reveal?
options: Atmospheric composition from hundreds of thousands of years ago
